bird.name: White-vented euphonia
bird.summary: The white-vented euphonia (Euphonia minuta) is a species of bird in the family Fringillidae, the finches and euphonias. It is found from Me…

locations.name: 亞馬遜盆地
locations.summary: The Amazon basin is the part of South America drained by the Amazon River and its tributaries.

locations.name: Várzea forest
locations.summary: A várzea forest is a seasonal floodplain forest inundated by whitewater rivers that occurs in the Amazon biome.

locations.name: 森林
locations.summary: 森林，是一个高密度树木的区域（或历史上，森林是一个为狩猎而留出的荒地），涵蓋大約9.4％的地球表面（或佔約30％的總土地面積）。聯合國糧食及農業組織(FAO)將森林定義為「面積超過 0.5 公頃、樹木超過 5 公尺且樹冠覆蓋率超過 10%…

relations.subject: White-vented euphonia
relations.evidence: There are also scattered records in Brazil to the east of its contiguous range. The white-vented euphonia inhabits a variety of humid to wet lowland forest landscapes in the tropical and lower subtropical zones. In Central America these include the canopy and edges of rainforest. In the Amazon Basin the landscapes include várzea and terra firme forests and nearby clearings in drier forest. In elevation it reaches in northern Central America, in Costa Rica, in Colombia, in Ecuador, in Peru, in Venezuela, and in Brazil.{{cite book | last =Schulenberg | first =T.S

relations.subject: White-vented euphonia
relations.evidence: No immediate threats have been identified. It is considered uncommon in northern Central America and Costa Rica, fairly common in Colombia, "generally scarce" in Ecuador, uncommon in Peru, fairly common in Venezuela, and "frequent to uncommon" in Brazil. Because of deforestation there, "[a]ll populations [west] of Andes are worthy of investigation
